results.title: Sept Visions Fugitives
results.material_description: Single channel moving image, black and white and colour and audio
results.brief_description: Sept Visions Fugitives was produced in China. Cahen creates impressionistic transformations of landscape and people. He writes: “The looking and listening audiences are confronted — in addition to the pure impressions — with basic questions about work (the boy using a traditional device to shake the feathers from the mattress) about freedom, about life in China … and ultimately about life and death: the burial, the bird, the man with a sheaf of straw on his back symbolizing the passage, the beyond ….. I had to struggle to make what I know are stereotyped images my own. The pure impressions and raw filmic material are completely reworked, going much further than the original takes.” Source: Electronic Arts Intermix [www.eai.org]

results.title: Computers in engineering
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Living with Computers”.
Martin Jones, a civil engineer, talks about the use of computers in engineering - a paper presented to the Junior World Computer Congress, Melbourne, October 1980.

results.title: Beginning silk screening on fabric (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Brenton Holmes, Open College of Further Education (S. Aust.) and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: This program demonstrates the basic procedures for silk-screening on to fabric using paper stencils. Building a frame, attaching the silk, preparing stencils and making a two-colour print are shown

results.title: The Brown dressing gown
results.brief_description: Patric fondly remembers his social worker Julie Wilson who helped him come out of his cocoon and discard his brown dressing gown. This Digital Storytelling project was created in partnership with the Department of Human Services and The Deafness Foundation.

results.title: What's the name of that place you come from nan?
results.creator_credit: Rita Hinekauia Morrison and ACMI Digital Storytelling
results.brief_description: Rita comes from a mixed cultural background but her strongest connection is with the Maori side of her family. She shares her memories and family history in this digital story so that her Maori heritage can be preserved through future generations.

results.title: Me and Chillelax
results.brief_description:
Helen lives at home with her parents but attends the WATCH centre (Waverley Adult Training Centre for the Handicapped) every day. It is through WATCH that Helen participates in a variety of educational and creative activities, has found new friends and now enjoys a social life outside her family home.
This project was funded by the Department of Human Services, (Eastern Metropolitan Region), developed and executed in partnership with MetroAccess at the City of Monash and City of Whitehorse, and Scope.

results.title: Life's great, thumbs up!
results.brief_description:
Edmund tells his story about family, his sporting interests including bike-riding, his involvement with Alkira (a centre for people with intellectual disabilities), his work as a volunteer at Box Hill Hospital, his love for music, and his role as a member of the L’Arche community.
This project was funded by the Department of Human Services, (Eastern Metropolitan Region), developed and executed in partnership with MetroAccess at the City of Monash and City of Whitehorse, and Scope.

results.title: Here and now
results.brief_description:
Depression can be a disability making every day seemingly a challenge just to get through to the next day. Brendan, with help and motivation, has overcome his struggle with depression and is enjoying the here and now - working, new friends and generally feeling good about himself. “Ode to Barry” written and performed by Brendan Forde.
This project was funded by the Department of Human Services, (Eastern Metropolitan Region), developed and executed in partnership with MetroAccess at the City of Monash and City of Whitehorse, and Scope.
